message:
test(ghw60): concurrent-reader crash-window E2E for atomic-swap publish contract

Bead coding_agent_session_search-ghw60 (child of ibuuh.10). Adds
tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs which exercises the atomic
lexical-index swap path (commit 109560e5 + a699f55b) end-to-end via
the real `cass index --full --force-rebuild` binary while a concurrent
reader thread polls `searchable_index_summary(&index_path)` in a
tight loop.

Test flow:
- Seed 1 Codex session, run `cass index --full` to populate live
  index and capture the stable doc count `before_docs`.
- Spawn a reader thread that polls the live index path every 1ms,
  recording each observation as `Result<Option<SearchableIndexSummary>,
  String>` until a shared stop flag or a 20s deadline trips.
- Trigger a rebuild via `cass index --full --force-rebuild --json`
  which exercises the staged-build + atomic-swap publish path.
- Join reader, assert post-publish summary read from the main thread
  equals before_docs (same corpus, just re-published).
- Assert every reader observation is legal: Ok(Some(before_docs)),
  Ok(Some(0)), Ok(None), or Err. Any other positive doc count would
  be a half-torn intermediate Tantivy directory.

FINDING surfaced by this test: the `Ok(Some(0))` carve-out is
load-bearing because `rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options` at
src/indexer/mod.rs:~11095 wipes the live index directory before the
new staged tree is populated, exposing a multi-second 0-doc window
to concurrent readers. This is a gap in the end-to-end atomic-swap
contract the commit message for 109560e5 implies. Filed as
coding_agent_session_search-9ct8r (MEDIUM/P2/bug). Once the
rebuild pre-wipe moves inside the atomic-swap publish logic, drop
the 0-doc carve-out here to enforce the stronger invariant.

Complements the sequential recovery guards landed in commit
19ebfb2e / b757d822 (all in-process, #[test] in src/indexer/mod.rs::tests):
- publish_staged_lexical_index_recovers_from_crash_between_park_and_swap
- publish_staged_lexical_index_retains_stale_in_progress_backup_when_live_present
- publish_staged_lexical_index_errors_cleanly_when_staged_path_does_not_exist
- recover_or_finalize_interrupted_lexical_publish_backup_is_idempotent_no_op

Validation: passes via
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cass_pane3 cargo test --test atomic_swap_publish_crash_window`
in 2.0s wall-clock (20s deadline was generous; actual publish
window is ~50ms).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
